title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear a brother and sister talking about a gift for their cousin.
extracts.audio_script: Man: What should we get Lucy for her new flat? || Woman: I saw a coffee machine online. It has good reviews. || Man: Why not buy it in town? || Woman: We could, but [8]the shop is on the other side of the city, and the buses there are terrible on Saturdays[/8]. It isn't heavy, and they do have it in stock. || Man: Then ordering online is easier. || Woman: Yes, and delivery is free this week. || Man: That sounds fair. || Woman: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What should we get Lucy for her new flat?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I saw a coffee machine online. It has good reviews.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Why not buy it in town?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: We could, but the shop is on the other side of the city, and the buses there are terrible on Saturdays. It isn't heavy, and they do have it in stock.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Then ordering online is easier.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and delivery is free this week.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds fair.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

questions.question: Why do they decide to buy the gift online?

options.A: It's heavy to carry home from the shop.
options.B: It's not available in the shop.
options.C: It's difficult to get to the shop.

questions.explanation: They choose online because the shop is far away and transport there is difficult.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues discussing their holiday travel plans.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: We're going to Scotland again this summer. || Man: By train as usual? || Woman: Yes, though the journey takes most of the day. || Man: Why not fly this time? || Woman: I don't like airports much, and the train station is near our hotel. || Man: [9]Still, if you flew, you could spend an extra afternoon by the lake instead of sitting on a train[/9]. || Woman: That's true, but I enjoy watching the countryside from the window. || Man: I hadn't thought of that. || Woman: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: We're going to Scotland again this summer.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: By train as usual?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, though the journey takes most of the day.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Why not fly this time?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I don't like airports much, and the train station is near our hotel.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Still, if you flew, you could spend an extra afternoon by the lake instead of sitting on a train.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's true, but I enjoy watching the countryside from the window.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I hadn't thought of that.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

questions.question: The man thinks that the woman should

options.A: go somewhere new for her holiday.
options.C: take a different type of transport.

questions.explanation: He says flying would give her an extra afternoon at the destination.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking to a friend about a fitness training session.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I can't come to training tonight, I'm afraid. || Woman: Is your cold still bad? || Man: No, that's gone now. And the operation on my knee isn't until next month. || Woman: So what's happened? || Man: [10]I saw the doctor this morning because my ankle still hurts, and she told me not to exercise for a few days[/10]. || Woman: Sensible advice. || Man: Yes. I'll watch the session online if they record it. || Woman: That must be useful. || Man: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I can't come to training tonight, I'm afraid.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is your cold still bad?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No, that's gone now. And the operation on my knee isn't until next month.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what's happened?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I saw the doctor this morning because my ankle still hurts, and she told me not to exercise for a few days.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Sensible advice.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. I'll watch the session online if they record it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That must be useful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

questions.question: The man cannot attend today's session because

options.A: his doctor has advised him to rest.
options.B: he has not recovered from a cold yet.
options.C: he has just had an operation.

questions.explanation: He says the doctor told him not to exercise for a few days.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a play they've just seen.
extracts.audio_script: Man: What did you think of the play? || Woman: It started slowly, but the second half was much better. || Man: I liked the story from the beginning. || Woman: The costumes were unusual, but they suited the characters. [11]For me, the acting was the weakest part. I expected the main actor to be much more convincing[/11]. || Man: Really? I thought he was fine. || Woman: Maybe I'm too difficult to please. || Man: That sounds sensible. || Woman: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What did you think of the play?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It started slowly, but the second half was much better.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I liked the story from the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The costumes were unusual, but they suited the characters. For me, the acting was the weakest part. I expected the main actor to be much more convincing.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Really? I thought he was fine.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe I'm too difficult to please.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the play?
questions.explanation: She says the acting was the weakest part and the main actor was not convincing.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a film and its soundtrack.
extracts.audio_script: Man: What are you listening to? || Woman: The soundtrack from Star Road. Have you seen the film? || Man: No. Is the music quiet? I need something relaxing for studying. || Woman: Not really. Some songs make you want to dance. [12]The film was made for teenagers, but the music is fun for children and adults too[/12]. || Man: Better than the film? || Woman: I wouldn't say that. They work well together. || Man: I can see why. || Woman: Yes, it wasn't what I expected, but I learnt something useful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What are you listening to?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The soundtrack from Star Road. Have you seen the film?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No. Is the music quiet? I need something relaxing for studying.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. Some songs make you want to dance. The film was made for teenagers, but the music is fun for children and adults too.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Better than the film?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I wouldn't say that. They work well together.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I can see why.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, it wasn't what I expected, but I learnt something useful.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the soundtrack?
questions.explanation: She says the music is fun for children and adults.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about the news.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Why are you checking your phone again? || Woman: I'm reading the news. I do it every morning before breakfast. || Man: I read headlines at work, but only if I have time. || Woman: [13]For me, finding out what's happening is part of my daily routine, like making coffee[/13]. || Man: I prefer sports pages, to be honest. || Woman: I read those too, but I like knowing the main stories first. || Man: Good idea. || Woman: I'll do that, and then I won't have to worry about it again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Why are you checking your phone again?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'm reading the news. I do it every morning before breakfast.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I read headlines at work, but only if I have time.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: For me, finding out what's happening is part of my daily routine, like making coffee.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I prefer sports pages, to be honest.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I read those too, but I like knowing the main stories first.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good idea.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'll do that, and then I won't have to worry about it again.

questions.question: They agree that

options.A: online news is always the most reliable.
options.B: there is too much news about sport.
options.C: reading the news is an essential part of the day.

questions.explanation: The woman says reading the news is part of her daily routine, and the man says he reads headlines at work.
